text: When designing REST APIs, following best practices ensures they are easy to use and maintain. First, choose clear and consistent endpoint names, such as `/users` or `/orders`, rather than confusing ones like `/getUserData`. Always use appropriate HTTP status codes to indicate success (200), client errors (404, 400), or server problems (500). Additionally, version your API from the start, for example `/v1/users`. This allows you to make changes without breaking existing users. Finally, provide thorough documentation that explains endpoints, request formats, and possible responses. Well-documented APIs save developers time and reduce mistakes.
